infobox.value: ([Ukrainian](/wiki/Ukrainian_language "Ukrainian language"): Донецький академічний державний театр опери та балету імені Анатолія Солов'яненка) was established in 1932 in [Donetsk](/wiki/Donetsk "Donetsk") on the basis of fit-up theatre of [Right-bank Ukraine](/wiki/Right-bank_Ukraine "Right-bank Ukraine"). Since 15 March 1932 the theatre was transferred to Donetsk theatre group[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) The first season opened on September 1, 1932, with opera *[Prince Igor](/wiki/Prince_Igor "Prince Igor")* composed by [Alexander Borodin](/wiki/Alexander_Borodin "Alexander Borodin").[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2) On April 12, 1941, the Theatre opened the season in the new theater building by premiere of [Mikhail Glinka's](/wiki/Mikhail_Glinka "Mikhail Glinka") *[Ivan Susanin](/wiki/A_Life_for_the_Tsar "A Life for the Tsar")*.[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2)[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) On August 7, same year, the premiere of the first ballet performance *[Laurencia](/wiki/Laurencia_(ballet) "Laurencia (ballet)")* by [Alexander Crain](/wiki/Alexander_Crain "Alexander Crain") was held.

Fedko, Alexey (4 July 2008). ["Донецкий академический государственный театр оперы и балета имени А.Б. Соловьяненко"](http://infodon.org.ua/time/252) [Donetsk State Academic Opera and Ballet Theater named after A.B. Solovyanenko]. *infodon.org.ua* (in Russian).

["Знаменитый Отелло начинал как машинист паровоза"](http://www.classicalmusicnews.ru/articles/Znamenityi-Otello-nachinal-kak-mashinist-parovoza/) [The famous Othello began as a locomotive driver]. *classicalmusicnews.ru* (in Russian). 5 December 2007.

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [48°00′22″N 37°48′10″E﻿ / ﻿48.0061°N 37.8028°E﻿ / 48.0061; 37.8028](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Donetsk_State_Academic_Opera_and_Ballet_Theatre&params=48.0061_N_37.8028_E_source:wikidata)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Donetsk State Academic Opera and Ballet Theatre named after A. Solovyanenko](/w/index.php?title=Donetsk_State_Academic_Opera_and_Ballet_Theatre_named_after_A._Solovyanenko&redirect=no "Donetsk State Academic Opera and Ballet Theatre named after A. Solovyanenko"))

Theatre in Donetsk, Ukraine

Donetsk State Academic Opera and Ballet Theatre

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Донецький академічний державний театр опери та балету імені Анатолія Солов'яненка | |
|  | |
| Map Interactive map of Donetsk State Academic Opera and Ballet Theatre | |
| Address | Artema street, 82 [Donetsk](/wiki/Donetsk "Donetsk"), [Donetsk Oblast](/wiki/Donetsk_Oblast "Donetsk Oblast") [Ukraine](/wiki/Ukraine "Ukraine") |
| Capacity | 976 seats |
| Construction | |
| Opened | 12 April 1941 |

**Donetsk State Academic Opera and Ballet Theatre named after A. Solovyanenko** ([Ukrainian](/wiki/Ukrainian_language "Ukrainian language"): Донецький академічний державний театр опери та балету імені Анатолія Солов'яненка) was established in 1932 in [Donetsk](/wiki/Donetsk "Donetsk") on the basis of fit-up theatre of [Right-bank Ukraine](/wiki/Right-bank_Ukraine "Right-bank Ukraine"). Since 15 March 1932 the theatre was transferred to Donetsk theatre group[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) The first season opened on September 1, 1932, with opera *[Prince Igor](/wiki/Prince_Igor "Prince Igor")* composed by [Alexander Borodin](/wiki/Alexander_Borodin "Alexander Borodin").[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2) On April 12, 1941, the Theatre opened the season in the new theater building by premiere of [Mikhail Glinka's](/wiki/Mikhail_Glinka "Mikhail Glinka") *[Ivan Susanin](/wiki/A_Life_for_the_Tsar "A Life for the Tsar")*.[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2)[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) On August 7, same year, the premiere of the first ballet performance *[Laurencia](/wiki/Laurencia_(ballet) "Laurencia (ballet)")* by [Alexander Crain](/wiki/Alexander_Crain "Alexander Crain") was held.

After the outbreak of the [Great Patriotic War](/wiki/Eastern_Front_(World_War_II) "Eastern Front (World War II)") part of the company was evacuated to [Kirghizia](/wiki/Kirghiz_Soviet_Socialist_Republic "Kirghiz Soviet Socialist Republic"), later, in June 1942, the theater moved to [Przhevalsk](/wiki/Karakol "Karakol") city, where actors held concerts in [hospitals](/wiki/Hospitals "Hospitals") and [military units](/wiki/Military_units "Military units").[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) In January 1944 the theater returned to [Donetsk](/wiki/Donetsk "Donetsk") and already in September, right after the liberation of the [Donets Basin](/wiki/Donets_Basin "Donets Basin") a premiere of Alexander Borodin's *[Prince Igor](/wiki/Prince_Igor "Prince Igor")* took place.

On October 2, 1977, the Theater was awarded with Academic status for significant contribution to the development of [Soviet art](/wiki/Soviet_art "Soviet art").
In 1992 the Choreographic School of [Vadym Pysarev](/wiki/Vadym_Pysarev "Vadym Pysarev") was established in the Theater. Starting from 1994 the Theater has been hosting the International Festival "World Ballet Stars", Vadim Pisarev is as well the founder and art director.

On December 9, 1999, by the Resolution of the [Cabinet of Ministers of Ukraine](/wiki/Cabinet_of_Ministers_of_Ukraine "Cabinet of Ministers of Ukraine"), the theater was named after Soviet Ukrainian opera singer [Anatoliy Solovyanenko](/wiki/Anatoliy_Solovyanenko "Anatoliy Solovyanenko").[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/31/1941-1943._%D0%9E%D0%BF%D0%B5%D1%80%D0%BD%D1%8B%D0%B9_%D1%82%D0%B5%D0%B0%D1%82%D1%80_%D0%B2%D0%BE_%D0%B2%D1%80%D0%B5%D0%BC%D1%8F_%D0%BE%D0%BA%D0%BA%D1%83%D0%BF%D0%B0%D1%86%D0%B8%D0%B8.jpg/250px-1941-1943._%D0%9E%D0%BF%D0%B5%D1%80%D0%BD%D1%8B%D0%B9_%D1%82%D0%B5%D0%B0%D1%82%D1%80_%D0%B2%D0%BE_%D0%B2%D1%80%D0%B5%D0%BC%D1%8F_%D0%BE%D0%BA%D0%BA%D1%83%D0%BF%D0%B0%D1%86%D0%B8%D0%B8.jpg)](/wiki/File:1941-1943._%D0%9E%D0%BF%D0%B5%D1%80%D0%BD%D1%8B%D0%B9_%D1%82%D0%B5%D0%B0%D1%82%D1%80_%D0%B2%D0%BE_%D0%B2%D1%80%D0%B5%D0%BC%D1%8F_%D0%BE%D0%BA%D0%BA%D1%83%D0%BF%D0%B0%D1%86%D0%B8%D0%B8.jpg)

Donetsk Opera Theatre during Nazi occupation, 1941-1943

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/71/Opera_theatre_in_donetsk_016.jpg/250px-Opera_theatre_in_donetsk_016.jpg)](/wiki/File:Opera_theatre_in_donetsk_016.jpg)

The Donetsk Opera Theatre in 2008

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/77/Donetsk_cinderella_01.jpg/250px-Donetsk_cinderella_01.jpg)](/wiki/File:Donetsk_cinderella_01.jpg)

Cinderella performance

## Architecture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Donetsk_State_Academic_Opera_and_Ballet_Theatre&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Architecture")]

The theatre building was initially designed for drama theatre, construction works started in 1936. Ludwig Kotovskiy was the leading architect and overall management of construction project was scheduled by Solomon Krol'.
The theater building is made in classic style. On three sides approaches to the theater are organized. The [auditorium](/wiki/Auditorium "Auditorium") and the [lobby](/wiki/Lobby_(room) "Lobby (room)") are decorated with [stucco](/wiki/Stucco "Stucco") details. Initially, the auditorium was designed for 1300 seats, at the present time there are 976.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Above the mezzanine and balcony of the auditorium, as well as in some niches of the foyer there are sculptural busts of composers, poets and decorative vases.
The theater is equipped with mechanized stage, the main stage area is 560 m2. It can withstand the load of up to 75 tons.

## The troupe

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Donetsk_State_Academic_Opera_and_Ballet_Theatre&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: The troupe")]

Many famous artists started their career in Donetsk Opera and Ballet Theatre, among them being [Yuri Gulayev](/wiki/Yuri_Aleksandrovich_Gulyayev "Yuri Aleksandrovich Gulyayev"), [Anatoliy Solovyanenko](/wiki/Anatoliy_Solovyanenko "Anatoliy Solovyanenko"), Nickolay Momot,[[4]](#cite_note-4) Valentin Zemlyanskiy,[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) [Alexander Korobeychenko](/w/index.php?title=Alexander_Korobeychenko&action=edit&redlink=1 "Alexander Korobeychenko (page does not exist)") and others - they were the People's artists of Ukraine.[[2]](#cite_note-:1-2)[[6]](#cite_note-6) The Theatre was named after prominent opera tenor Anatoliy Solovyanenko, awardee of [Taras Shevchenko](/wiki/Taras_Shevchenko_National_Prize "Taras Shevchenko National Prize") and [Lenin Prizes](/wiki/Lenin_Prize "Lenin Prize"), who was born in Donetsk. In different years on the stage of Donetsk Opera and Ballet Theatre such stars of [Soviet Union](/wiki/Soviet_Union "Soviet Union") art danced and sang, as [Ivan Kozlovsky](/wiki/Ivan_Kozlovsky "Ivan Kozlovsky") and [Sergei Lemeshev](/wiki/Sergei_Lemeshev "Sergei Lemeshev"), [Maria Bieshu](/wiki/Maria_Bieshu "Maria Bieshu"), [Olga Lepeshinskaya](/wiki/Olga_Lepeshinskaya_(dancer) "Olga Lepeshinskaya (dancer)"), [Marina Semenova](/wiki/Marina_Semenova "Marina Semenova"), [Klavdiya Shulzhenko](/wiki/Klavdiya_Shulzhenko "Klavdiya Shulzhenko") and many others.
The troupe of the Theatre included as of 2014:

### Management

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Donetsk_State_Academic_Opera_and_Ballet_Theatre&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Management")]

* Director - Honoured Artist of Ukraine Vasiliy Ryabenkiy
* Art Director - People's Artist of Ukraine [Vadim Pisarev](/wiki/Vadim_Pisarev "Vadim Pisarev")
* [Chief Choirmaster](/wiki/Choirmaster "Choirmaster") - People's Artist of Ukraine Ludmila Streltsova
* [Chief choreographer](/wiki/Choreographer "Choreographer") - People's Artist of Ukraine Evgeniya Hasyanova
* [Chief conductor](/wiki/Conductor_(music) "Conductor (music)") - Honoured Artist of Ukraine Vasily Vasilenko

### Opera
